message:
style(fuzz): rustfmt sweep across four existing fuzz targets

Mechanical rustfmt cleanups on the four fuzz targets that existed
before this wave of fuzz work. No behavior change. Split out from
the substantive new-target commit so reviewers can eyeball the
style bits separately.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_command_parse.rs
  * Import sort: `{parse_frame, RespFrame}` →
    `{RespFrame, parse_frame}` matching workspace "types before
    functions" convention.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_function_restore.rs
  * `store.function_load(...).is_ok()` one-liner broken across
    multiple lines so the receiver and call arguments each get
    their own line per the line-length policy.
  * `body.split_first().map_or((0, &[][..]), |(&head, tail)| (head,
    tail))` reflowed onto a single fluent chain instead of the
    prior nested-map form.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_glob_match.rs
  * Rustfmt-only reflow of the existing argument-builder and
    input-decoder blocks. No semantic change.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_runtime_sequence.rs (+122 lines / -30 net)
  * Large mechanical reflow of every `FuzzCommand::Foo { key,
    value, … }` enum variant so each field lives on its own line
    instead of trying to fit in one. Rustfmt's "struct-literal-in-
    enum-variant" policy for the workspace; `git diff -w` across
    this file is empty.

No corpus file or logic changed — this commit is purely whitespace
and field-layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
